title: chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. yezoense
intro: Chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. yezoense, also known as the Yezo chrysanthemum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and gray-green leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory infections. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. yezoense include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy in Asian cultures. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine formulas, such as those used to treat bronchitis and sinusitis.
